product_scope: itpay is the single public CLI entry point, and $itpay is its user-facing Skill invocation. Under that one product entry point, the two top-level commerce actions are buy and sell: Buyer workflows are available now; Seller workflows will use the same entry point and are not implemented yet.
title: Order, Delivery And Refund Recovery
purpose: Read owned orders and manage refunds through signed Agent authority without guessing payment or refund state.
when_to_use: An order or Service Execution was interrupted after payment.
The buyer asks to request, track, or cancel a refund.

commands.success_signal: one compact owned-order summary and one current next step are returned
the Refund Owner returns its request id, policy status, consumption state, and access lock
one authoritative refund snapshot is returned
one terminal refund envelope or one resumable timeout envelope is returned
the Refund Owner cancels the request and requires a new delivery authorization

agent_rules: The Backend derives payment, amount, currency, Buyer and refund policy from the owned order.
A refund request freezes all delivery paths and revokes existing grants immediately.
Cancellation or rejection restores eligibility but never reactivates an old grant.
Use get or watch after interruption; do not infer success from elapsed time.
Account-wide orders requires an account-scoped Buyer bearer; exact order and refund routes support the bound signed Agent where documented.
forbidden: Do not fabricate Buyer sessions, Device IDs or payment references.
Do not read delivery while refund access_locked is true.
Do not claim a refund succeeded before the Refund Owner says succeeded.
